perItemFindings.label: thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery
perItemFindings.one_line: I-1 apolarity/catalecticant machinery and I-2 Zariski/real-open theory are named, absent whole theories—not a bounded handful of lemmas—yet the plan leaves the headline as a deferred theorem rather than threading named external assumptions.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-separation
perItemFindings.one_line: Its essential apolar certificate (I-1) is research-scale absent substrate; a deferred crux does not satisfy the required external-theory routing, and its conditional result must expose that assumption to consumers.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-exceptional-locus
perItemFindings.one_line: The proposed proof requires absent image-variety dimension/codimension and apolarity apparatus (I-1/I-2), explicitly described as multi-file external theory, so it must be conditional on named substrate rather than merely deferred.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:minimal-real-information-order
perItemFindings.one_line: The full truncated Hamburger moment problem (I-4), alongside apolarity (I-1), is an absent named theory; reclassify only those irreducible facts as assumptions and thread them, including through the explicit oeq:generic-separation dependency.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:real-exceptional-atlas
perItemFindings.one_line: RealExceptionalAtlas assumes the entire paper-specific finite-atlas conclusion, fusing CAD/QE and truncated-Hamburger external facts with derivable feasibility/incidence/elimination reductions; the gate must contain only separate irreducible external primitives.

perItemFindings.label: def:global-feasible-fiber-decision / def:direction-selector / def:real-atlas-handle
perItemFindings.one_line: The core promises QE-based exact feasibility and an evaluable finite semialgebraic selector, but the plan retains only relational Props while declaring the CAD and moment substrate standalone; the conditional evaluability/exact-feasibility claims need explicitly threaded named interfaces.

verbatim_critique: Size I-1, I-2, and I-4 as research-scale external theories and replace the affected deferred headlines by conditional theorem assemblies over narrowly stated named assumptions; thread those assumptions through every dependent result. Split RealExceptionalAtlas into at least a generic real-QE/CAD correctness interface and a truncated-Hamburger atomic-representation interface, then prove/plan the project-specific feasibility, incidence, and atlas reductions separately. Thread the QE interface to every claim of finite/evaluable selection and the moment interface to every claimed equivalence with real source feasibility.
